seo.title: Building Code Generation with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation agents with OpenAI's Agents SDK enables powerful iterative development workflows where LLMs generate, test, and refine code in sandboxes. However, the high-risk nature of executing generated code demands robust guardrails to prevent security vulnerabilities, infinite loops, and credential leaks. Vex integration provides runtime verification that ensures safe code execution and validated agent handoffs.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture features a Planner Agent that decomposes coding tasks, a CodeGenerator Agent that produces Python code, an Executor Agent that runs code in a sandbox, and a Validator Agent that checks test results and safety. Agents hand off via the SDK's structured messaging, with tracing for debugging multi-step workflows. This creates tight feedback loops for iterative refinement but exposes risks in unvalidated handoffs and execution.

Key components include the Runner orchestrating agent execution, custom tools for sandboxing (e.g., E2B or Docker), and guardrails at each handoff point. Vex integrates as output parsers to validate agent responses before consumption by downstream agents.

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Planner and CodeGenerator agents enter infinite refinement cycles without convergence criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex convergence checks on agent outputs before handoff

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious user inputs trick code generator into producing dangerous syscalls or credential exfiltration
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and output content policy enforcement

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: Executor returns malformed test results that confuse Validator agent
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation on all tool outputs

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Failed handoffs between sandbox executor and validator lose context
risks.mitigation: Vex structured tracing spans across agent boundaries

implementation.title: Basic Code Generation Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize OpenAI Agents SDK with code generation and sandbox execution tools
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner, function_tool
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

@function_tool
async def execute_code(code: str) -> str:
    # Sandbox execution (E2B/Docker)
    result = sandbox.run(code)
    return f"Output: {result.stdout}\nError: {result.stderr}"

code_agent = Agent(
    name="Code Generator",
    instructions="Generate Python code that solves the task. Include tests.",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[execute_code]
)

runner = Runner(agents=[code_agent])

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Handoff Chain
implementation.description: Create planner → generator → executor workflow with handoffs
implementation.code:
planner = Agent(
    name="Task Planner",
    instructions="Break down coding task into code generation + testing steps",
    model="gpt-4o",
    handoffs_to=[code_agent]
)

validator = Agent(
    name="Test Validator",
    instructions="Check if tests pass and code is safe. Request fixes if needed",
    model="gpt-4o",
    handoffs_to=[code_agent]
)

multi_agent_runner = Runner(agents=[planner, code_agent, validator])

implementation.title: Sandbox Tool with Dependency Control
implementation.description: Secure execution environment with whitelisted dependencies

implementation.title: Iterative Refinement Loop
implementation.description: Run agents until validation succeeds or max iterations reached
implementation.code:
async def generate_code(task: str, max_iterations: int = 5):
    result = await multi_agent_runner.run(task)
    
    for i in range(max_iterations):
        if result.final_output.get('tests_passed', False):
            return result.final_output
        
        # Trigger refinement
        feedback = result.final_output['feedback']
        result = await multi_agent_runner.run(f"Fix: {feedback}")
    
    return result.final_output

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail on Code Agent
vexIntegration.description: Validate generated code for safety and syntax before executor consumption
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail, Policy

code_safety_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="code-safety",
    policies=[
        Policy.CodeSafety(),
        Policy.NoCredentials(),
        Policy.SyntaxValid(language="python"),
        Policy.NoExecCalls()
    ]
)

code_agent = Agent(
    name="Code Generator",
    instructions="...",
    output_guardrails=[code_safety_guardrail],
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[execute_code]
)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Handoff Validation Between Agents
vexIntegration.description: Ensure structured data flows correctly between planner and generator

vexIntegration.title: Vex Tracing for Multi-Agent Workflows
vexIntegration.description: Complete visibility across agent handoffs and tool calls
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# Initialize Vex tracing
vex.init(project="code-gen-agents")

async def traced_workflow(task: str):
    with vex.span("full-code-generation"):
        with vex.span("planner"):
            plan_result = await planner.run(task)
        
        with vex.span("code-gen"):
            code_result = await code_agent.run(plan_result)
            
        with vex.span("validation"):
            final = await validator.run(code_result)
            
        vex.span.set_attribute("tests_passed", final.get('tests_passed', False))
        return final

# Auto-instruments all agent calls
